LFC (Looking For Config)
Canonical URL: https://lfc.dev/

Summary
LFC is a service for teams that need consistent AI tool configuration across many machines. Admins define MCP servers, shared skills, instructions, rules, and secrets once; LFC syncs the managed parts into supported tools without overwriting personal config.

What LFC does
- Manages the files AI tools already read from disk.
- Syncs approved MCP servers, skills, instructions, rules, and secrets to developer machines.
- Preserves personal config and only updates entries managed by LFC.
- Gives teams a dashboard for profiles, rollout, audit history, and fleet visibility.
- Supports bottom-up suggestions: developers can submit useful local tools or skills for admin review.

Who it is for
- Engineering teams standardizing AI coding and desktop tools.
- Admins who want one source of truth for AI tool setup.
- Developers who need fast onboarding and consistent local environments.

Supported tools mentioned on the public site
- Claude Code
- Cursor
- Claude Desktop
- Codex
- Windsurf
- OpenCode

Managed artifact types
- MCP servers
- Shared skills
- Instructions
- Rules
- Secrets

Core workflow
1. Define profiles with the MCPs, skills, instructions, rules, and secrets your team should use.
2. Install the LFC client, which detects supported tools on the machine.
3. Sync approved configuration to those tools while leaving personal entries untouched.

Safety model
- LFC only touches entries marked as managed by LFC.
- Personal configs outside the managed scope are preserved.
- Secrets resolve at install or write time rather than being pasted into shared docs.

Public pages
- https://lfc.dev/ : marketing overview and pricing
- https://lfc.dev/register : sign-up
- https://lfc.dev/login : sign-in
- https://lfc.dev/privacy : privacy policy
- https://lfc.dev/terms : terms of service
- https://lfc.dev/acceptable-use : acceptable use policy

Key product messages from the site
- "Same AI tools. Same config. Every machine."
- "Define your team's MCP servers, skills, instructions, and rules once."
- "Your configs are safe."

Operator
LFC.DEV is operated by Abrega, Inc.

Contact
- privacy@lfc.dev
- legal@lfc.dev
- abuse@lfc.dev
